title: Add visible Get Location button and change default position to Spain
description: The app currently defaults to Marseille, France and only tries geolocation silently on page load — which fails in iframe/preview contexts. We need a visible button to trigger geolocation and a sensible default for a user in Spain.

plan.summary: Fix two UX issues: (1) Change the default boat position from Marseille, France to Barcelona, Spain (41.39°N, 2.17°E) which is a major sailing hub. (2) Add a visible '📍 My Location' button to the map UI that triggers the browser's geolocation API on click, so users in iframe/preview contexts can still locate themselves. The button will be styled to match the existing dark nautical theme and placed near the refresh button at the bottom-right.

steps.description: Update sailing-weather.js: change default boatPosition from {lat: 43.3, lon: 5.37} (Marseille) to {lat: 41.39, lon: 2.17} (Barcelona, Spain). Add a 'Get Location' button click handler that calls navigator.geolocation.getCurrentPosition and moves the boat marker + refreshes weather. Keep the silent tryGeolocation on load as a bonus, but the button is the primary UX path. (req 1, 2)
Update sailing-app template: add a 'Get Location' button element (📍 icon) next to the refresh button at the bottom-right. (req 2)
Update sailing-weather.css: add styles for the new location button — matching the refresh button's nautical dark theme with hover/active states. (req 2)
Screenshot and verify both changes are visible and the app loads centered on Spain with the new button visible. (req 1, 2)
